schedulingPolicy: Every concept receives immediate model, guided, faded, debug, independent-transfer, and familiar-assessment family references inside its owner module before later evidence is scheduled.

Next-use references prefer an explicitly named module retrieval, then a downstream prerequisite use, then a deliberate interleaved review; final-module evidence proceeds to the certification examination.
Delayed retrieval prefers a semantically relevant use at least two modules later; otherwise it enters a review three modules later or the certification examination near the course boundary.
Initial 3, 14, and 45 day due points are scheduling seeds only; future intervals adapt from correctness, attempts, hints, latency, confidence, accessibility needs, and correction evidence.
Failure routes to a smaller model, a parallel faded task, and a different variant from the assessment bank; it never reveals canonical answers or grants progression from response length.
architectureFindings: All 186 concepts now have explicit I-G-F-debug-transfer-assessment references, next-use references, delayed retrieval references, correction routes, and certification-strand ownership.

Next-use basis distribution is {"named-module-retrieval":75,"planned-interleaving":91,"downstream-prerequisite":19,"certification-exam":1}; delayed basis distribution is {"delayed:named-downstream-use":71,"delayed:scheduled-interleaved-review":108,"delayed:certification-exam":7}.
This matrix links planning identities only. It does not create learner-facing explanation, checks, schedules, item variants, or observed mastery.
gaps: Each referenced activity still needs reviewed concept clusters and original learner-facing steps, examples, checks, three-stage hints, failure feedback, correction variants, and cumulative validation.
Named delays remain planning positions until the persistence engine records due events, real attempt evidence, adaptive scheduling decisions, and successful delayed performance.
Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, runtime, duplication, and representative-learner review remains open.
No record may advance from planned-not-authored because this static trace is structurally complete.
